revision.text:
[[ja:石刀神社]] {{Expand Japanese|石刀神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Iwato Shrine | native_name = 石刀神社 | image = Iwato shrine.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Iwato Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.327933|136.797136}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|fr|Tajikarao|it|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|アメノタヂカラオ|ko|아메노타지카라오|ru|Амэ-но Тадзикарао|simple|Ame-no-Tajikarao|zh|天手力男神|qid=Q849412|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} | website = {{Official website|https://www.iwatojinja.com/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Iwato Shrine'''|石刀神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11465292|ltq=Q11465292|qid=Q11465292|1=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province}} ** Series ordinal: 20 ** P155: {{ill|Q11459557|ltq=Q11459557|qid=Q11459557|1=Ozeki Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Muroharano Shrine|lt=Muroharano Shrine|qid=Q135039473|1=Muroharano Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q270810|ltq=Q270810|qid=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}} * {{ill|Q514506|ltq=Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} * {{ill|Nakashima district|lt=Nakashima district|ja|中島郡|zh|中島郡|qid=Q780420|1=Nakashima district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * Тадзикарао|simple|Ame-no-Tajikarao|zh|天手力男神|qid=Q849412|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181361 ** P3831: ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いわとじんじゃ == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Ihatono Shrine|lt=Ihatono Shrine|qid=Q135039471|1=Ihatono Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市今伊勢町馬寄石刀2', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:石刀神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11585184}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年10月26日 (日) 04:25 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称=Iwato Shrine | 画像=[[File:Iwato shrine.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|qid=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}} 2 Umayose-Iwato, Imaise-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|40.62|N|136|47|49.84|E|display=ti}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|qid=Q849412|lt=Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}・{{ill|qid=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi|Q478504|qid=Q478504|qid=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}}・{{ill|lt=Toyokumono|Q4387158|qid=Q4387158|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto}}・Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} | 創建=Unknown | 例祭=April 19 and the following Sunday | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Iwato Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} [[File:石刀神社 (愛知県一宮市今伊勢町) - panoramio.jpg|220px|thumb|right|Torii]] '''Iwato Shrine''' (いわとじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Imaise-cho, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|qid=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}} (formerly {{ill|lt=Nakajima District (Aichi)|Q24875390|qid=Q24875390|lt=Nakajima District|1=Nakashima District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}). It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}. At the annual "Iwato Festival" held each April, three {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11470974|qid=Q11470974|1=Matsuri float}} are pulled. As for the Engishiki shrine "Iwato Shrine," there are competing theories: in addition to this shrine in Imaise-cho, candidates include the "Iwato Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Asai-cho (Aichi)|Q11556970|qid=Q11556970|lt=Asai-cho|1=Azai}}, Ichinomiya, and the "Yatsurugi Shrine" in Kuwabara-cho, {{ill|lt=Hashima|Q819595|qid=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. The Hashima shrine is conjectured to be an old site of this shrine. For the Asai-cho shrine, see {{ill|lt=Iwato Shrine#Iwato Shrine (Ichinomiya, Asai-cho)|Q11585184|qid=Q11585184|lt=Iwato Shrine (Ichinomiya, Asai-cho)|1=Iwato Shrine}}. === Overview === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|qid=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. The original site is traditionally said to have been in "Shijo" of Nakajima District, Owari Province. The place name "Sanjo" remains in Ichinomiya today, and the area north of it toward Hashima in Gifu is considered likely. It is said to have later been moved near the current site by {{ill|lt=Tachibana Kachiko|Q1058216|qid=Q1058216|lt=Empress Danrin|1=Tachibana no Kachiko}} (consort of {{ill|lt=Emperor Saga|Q316654|qid=Q316654|1=Emperor Saga}}). The final move to the current location is said to have been around the {{ill|lt=14th century|Q7034|qid=Q7034|1=14th century}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was renamed "Sanmyojin Shrine" after combining a branch of {{ill|lt=Ise Jingu|Q687168|qid=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. This area had long been a {{ill|lt=jinko (religious households)|Q11589711|qid=Q11589711|1=Kanbe (social class)}} area of Ise Jingu. In {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|qid=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|qid=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} visited and worshiped while marching west. Because he set up camp at Sanmyojin Shrine, shrine buildings were damaged and part of the structures dismantled. After the {{ill|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|Q234188|qid=Q234188|1=Battle of Sekigahara}}, it was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1608|Q6758|qid=Q6758|1=1608}} (Keicho 13). The Iwato Festival is said to have begun to celebrate this rebuilding. In {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|qid=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13), it was renamed Iwato Shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|qid=Q18633|1=1940}} (Showa 15) it became a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === === Main deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|qid=Q849412|lt=Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} * {{ill|lt=Toyokumono|Q4387158|qid=Q4387158|lt=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|qid=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi|Q478504|qid=Q478504|qid=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} * Kunikuzuchi-no-Mikoto === Combined deities === * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} * {{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-bime no Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Kikurihime|Q11619891|qid=Q11619891|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kikurihime}} * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and others === Cultural properties === === Aichi Prefecture designated cultural property === * Iwato Festival (intangible folk) === Ichinomiya City designated cultural properties === * Iwato Shrine festival floats (tangible folk) * Sutra cylinder (craft) - inscribed {{ill|lt=1396|Q6405|qid=Q6405|1=1396}} ({{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|qid=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 3) * Hanging Buddha (craft) - inscribed {{ill|lt=1368|Q6207|qid=Q6207|1=1368}} ({{ill|lt=Oan|Q1146245|qid=Q1146245|1=Ōan}} 1) * Hanging lantern - Edo period === Address === * 2 Umayose-Iwato, Imaise-cho, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture === Transportation === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Iwato Station|Q11585186|qid=Q11585186|1=Iwato Station}} on the Meitetsu {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|qid=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}. * {{ill|lt=i-Bus|Q11223722|qid=Q11223722|1=I-Bus}}: get off at "Umayose" bus stop on the Ichinomiya course; about 12 minutes on foot. === Iwato Festival === A festival of Iwato Shrine in which {{ill|lt=festival floats|Q11470974|qid=Q11470974|1=Matsuri float}} are pulled on the Sunday immediately after {{ill|lt=April 19|Q2523|qid=Q2523|1=April 19}} each year. Before WWII there were five floats, but two were lost in {{ill|lt=air raids|Q2380335|qid=Q2380335|1=Airstrike}} during the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|qid=Q184425|1=Pacific War}}, leaving three today. === Iwato Shrine (Ichinomiya, Asai-cho) === {{神社 | 名称=Iwato Shrine | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=一宮市}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|qid=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}} 271 Iwatoduka, Kuroiwa, Asai-cho | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|21|7.73|N|136|50|4.32|E|}} | 祭神=Yasokikurihime-no-kami | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Iwato Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|21|7.73|N|136|50|4.32|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220|coord={{coord|35|21|7.73|N|136|50|4.32|E}}}} }} Iwato Shrine (Asai-cho, Ichinomiya) is a shrine in Asai-cho, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. There is a theory that it is the Shikinaisha "Iwato Shrine" of Nakajima District, Owari Province. However, this area was {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|qid=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}} of Owari Province, so this theory is questioned. On the other hand, as {{ill|lt=Kosaka Shrine|Q11459557|qid=Q11459557|1=Ozeki Shrine}} in Asai-cho is also estimated to be an Engishiki shrine of Nakajima District, and because this area was frequently flooded by the {{ill|lt=Kiso River|Q779607|qid=Q779607|1=Kiso River}} and district boundaries in the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}} were unclear, {{独自研究範囲|it is possible this area was also in Nakajima District.|date=2021年5月}}. Every year on the first Saturday of August, the Kuroiwa Iwato Gion Festival is held and one festival float is pulled. On March 22, {{ill|lt=2016|Q25245|qid=Q25245|1=2016}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 28), it was designated an Ichinomiya City intangible folk cultural property. === Overview === It is said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|qid=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. The enshrined deity is said to be Yasokikurihime-no-kami, but even now there is no formal shrine building, and a giant rock is worshiped as the sacred body. The sacred giant rock is a glossy black boulder about 6 shaku long and 4 shaku wide, said to shine golden in sunlight; it is also called a胴体 rock. Outside the precinct is another giant rock (tail rock) of about 6 shaku square. Because rocks are the objects of worship, it may preserve an ancient worship form. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it is said to have been called "Kuroiwa Tenno." It is said to have been renamed Iwato Shrine in the Meiji era. === Transportation === * Get off at "Kawada Kuroiwa" bus stop on {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|qid=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}. ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|qid=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} / {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|qid=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}}, take bus bound for "Miyada-hongo" or "Kawashima". ** From {{ill|lt=Konan Station (Aichi)|Q201473|qid=Q201473|1=Kōnan Station (Aichi)}}, take bus bound for "Kawashima". *Note: there are two "Kawada Kuroiwa" bus stops. The nearest to the shrine is on the Meitetsu Ichinomiya - Miyada-hongo / Konan - Kawashima route (about 5 minutes on foot). From the Meitetsu Ichinomiya - Kawashima route stop, it is about 8 minutes on foot.* === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Iwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Iwato Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]]
